fields.name: Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the entry, as assigned from the specified number series when the entry was created.

fields.name: Item No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the item in the entry.

fields.name: Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the posting date for the entry.

fields.name: Entry Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies which type of transaction the entry is created from.

fields.name: Source No.
fields.tooltip: Shows where the entry originated. If the entry was posted from an item journal line, the field will be empty. If the entry was posted from an purchase order, purchase invoice or purchase credit memo, the field displays the buy-from vendor number. If it is posted from sales the sell-to customer number will be displayed.

fields.name: Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the document number on the entry. The document is the voucher that the entry was based on, for example, a receipt.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description of the entry.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the location that the entry is linked to.

fields.name: Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of units of the item in the item entry.

fields.name: Remaining Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity in the Quantity field that remains to be processed.

fields.name: Invoiced Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been invoiced.

fields.name: SIFT Bucket No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies an automatically generated number that is used by the system to enable better concurrency.

fields.name: Applies-to Entry
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the quantity on the journal line must be applied to an already-posted entry. In that case, enter the entry number that the quantity will be applied to.

fields.name: Open
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the entry has been fully applied to.

fields.name: Global Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the global dimension that is linked to the record or entry for analysis purposes. Two global dimensions, typically for the company's most important activities, are available on all cards, documents, reports, and lists.

fields.name: Global Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the global dimension that is linked to the record or entry for analysis purposes. Two global dimensions, typically for the company's most important activities, are available on all cards, documents, reports, and lists.

fields.name: Positive
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the item in the item ledge entry is positive.

fields.name: Source Type
fields.tooltip: Shows the source type that applies to the source number that is shown in the Source No. field. If the entry was posted from an item journal line, the field is blank. If posted from a sales line, the source type is Customer. If posted from a purchase line, the source type is Vendor. If the entry resulted from the production of a BOM (bill of materials), the source type is Item.

fields.name: Drop Shipment
fields.tooltip: Specifies if your vendor ships the items directly to your customer.

fields.name: Transaction Type
fields.tooltip:
Shows the code for the transaction type of the entry.
The field can be filled in from the following fields:
If the entry was posted from an item journal line, the transaction type is copied from the Transaction Type field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the transaction type is copied from the Transaction Type field on the sales or purchase line.
The field is used in reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: Transport Method
fields.tooltip:
Shows the code for the entry's transport method.
The field can be filled in from the following fields:

If the entry was posted from an item journal line, the code is copied from the Transport Method field on the journal line.


If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the code is copied from the Transport Method field on the sales or purchase line.


The field is used in reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip:
Shows the country/region code for the customer or vendor to which the item entry is linked.
The field can be filled in from the following fields:
If the entry was posted from an item journal line, the code is copied from the Country/Region Code field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the code is copied from the VAT Country/Region Code field on the sales or purchase header.
The country/region code is used in the reporting of INTRASTAT and EU VAT information.

fields.name: Entry/Exit Point
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code of either the port of entry at which the items passed into your country/region or the port of exit through which you shipped the items out of your country/region.
The program fills in the field in one of the following ways:
If the entry was posted from an item journal line, the code is copied from the Entry/Exit Point field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the code is copied from the Exit Point field on the sales header or the Entry Point field on the purchase header.

fields.name: Document Date
fields.tooltip:
Shows the date on the document that provided the basis for this item entry.
The program fills in the field in one of the following ways:
If the entry was posted from a journal line, the date is copied from the Document Date field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the date is copied from the Document Date field on the sales or purchase header.

fields.name: External Document No.
fields.tooltip:
Shows the external document number for this entry.
If the entry was posted from a journal line, the number is copied from the External Document No. field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from a sales order, invoice or credit memo, the number is copied from the External Document No. on the sales header.

fields.name: Area
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code for the area of the customer or vendor with which you traded the items in this entry.
The field can be filled in from the following fields:
If the entry was posted from an item journal line, the code is copied from the Area field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the code is copied from the Area field on the sales or purchase line.
The code is used for reporting to INTRASTAT and will appear on the Intrastat Journal.

fields.name: Transaction Specification
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code for the transaction specification of the entry.
The field can be filled in from the following fields:
If the entry was posted from an item journal line, the transaction specification code is copied from the Transaction Specification field on the journal line.
If the entry was posted from an order, invoice or credit memo, the code is copied from the Transaction Specification field on the sales or purchase line.
The code is used for reporting to INTRASTAT and will appear on the Intrastat Journal.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip: Contains the code for the number series to which the document number on this entry belongs.

fields.name: Reserved Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been reserved.

fields.name: Document Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies what type of document was posted to create the item ledger entry.

fields.name: Document Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the line on the posted document that corresponds to the item ledger entry.

fields.name: Order Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies which type of transaction the entry is created from.

fields.name: Order No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the order that created the entry.

fields.name: Order Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line number of the order that created the entry.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 3 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 3, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 4 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 4, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 5 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 5, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 6 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 6, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 7 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 7, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 8 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 8, which is one of dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Assemble to Order
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the posting represents an assemble-to-order sale.

fields.name: Job No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the related project.

fields.name: Job Task No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the related project task.

fields.name: Job Purchase
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the entry relates to a purchase for a job.

fields.name: Variant Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the variant of the item on the line.

fields.name: Qty. per Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity per item unit of measure.

fields.name: Unit of Measure Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how each unit of the item or resource is measured, such as in pieces or hours. By default, the value in the Base Unit of Measure field on the item or resource card is inserted.

fields.name: Derived from Blanket Order
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether you posted the entry from a sales order that is connected to a blanket sales order.
The program fills in this field automatically, and it is not editable.

fields.name: Originally Ordered No.
fields.tooltip: Contains the number of the item that was originally ordered.

fields.name: Originally Ordered Var. Code
fields.tooltip: Contains the code of the variant that was originally ordered.

fields.name: Out-of-Stock Substitution
fields.tooltip: Specifies that this the originally ordered item was out of stock.The program uses this field internally.

fields.name: Item Category Code
fields.tooltip: Contains the item category code for the item on this line.

fields.name: Nonstock
fields.tooltip:
Specifies that the item on the line is a nonstock item (an item not normally kept in inventory).
The program copies the contents of the nonstock item field from the Nonstock field in the Sales Line table.
You cannot make changes to the contents of the this because the shipment has already been posted.

fields.name: Purchasing Code
fields.tooltip:
Contains the purchasing code for the item, which indicates the actions that were taken to purchase the item.
The program copies the purchasing code from the Purchasing Code field in the Sales Line table.
You cannot make changes to the contents of the this field because the shipment has already been posted.

fields.name: Completely Invoiced
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the entry has been fully invoiced or if more posted invoices are expected. Only completely invoiced entries can be revalued.

fields.name: Last Invoice Date
fields.tooltip:
Shows the date of the latest posting of this entry. The date is used for revaluing items.
It is only used internally by the program.

fields.name: Applied Entry to Adjust
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether there is one or more applied entries, which need to be adjusted.

fields.name: Cost Amount (Expected)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the expected cost, in LCY, of the quantity posting.

fields.name: Cost Amount (Actual)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the adjusted cost, in LCY, of the quantity posting.

fields.name: Cost Amount (Non-Invtbl.)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the adjusted non-inventoriable cost, that is an item charge assigned to an outbound entry.

fields.name: Cost Amount (Expected) (ACY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the expected cost, in ACY, of the quantity posting.

fields.name: Cost Amount (Actual) (ACY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the adjusted cost of the entry, in the additional reporting currency.

fields.name: Cost Amount (Non-Invtbl.)(ACY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the adjusted non-inventoriable cost, that is, an item charge assigned to an outbound entry in the additional reporting currency.

fields.name: Purchase Amount (Expected)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of purchase amounts that are posted as received but not invoiced.

fields.name: Purchase Amount (Actual)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of purchase amounts that are posted as received and invoiced.

fields.name: Sales Amount (Expected)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the expected sales amount, in LCY.

fields.name: Sales Amount (Actual)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sales amount, in LCY.

fields.name: Correction
fields.tooltip:
Specifies if the item ledger entry was posted as a corrective entry, for example in connection with a return process or after using the Undo Receipt, Undo Shipment, Undo Assembly, or Undo Service Consumption functions.
In case of sales return, the value is copied from the Correction field on the returned sales order that has been reversed.

fields.name: Shipped Qty. Not Returned
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity for this item ledger entry that was shipped and has not yet been returned.

fields.name: Remaining Qty. by Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the remaining quantity for the item ledger entry as of the date specified in the Date Filter field.

fields.name: Prod. Order Comp. Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line number of the production order component.

fields.name: Serial No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a serial number if the posted item carries such a number.

fields.name: Lot No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a lot number if the posted item carries such a number.

fields.name: Warranty Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the last day of warranty for the item on the line.

fields.name: Expiration Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the last date that the item on the line can be used.

fields.name: Item Tracking
fields.tooltip:
This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use. The purpose of the field is to make it easier to filter the entries with item tracking.
The field is updated automatically. It specifies whether the field carries item tracking and if so, whether it carries a lot number, a serial number, or both a lot and serial number.

fields.name: Package No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a package number if the posted item carries such a number.

fields.name: Return Reason Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code explaining why the item was returned.

fields.name: Item Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies the description of the item in the entry. Analysis mode must be used for sorting and filtering on this field.
